judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with well-grounded, repo-specific evidence; they are comparable, with Report 1 and Report 2 each adding distinct extras (strict value coercion vs. UI/backend default mismatch).

report_1.evidence: Reminder messages **will be sent automatically**. The deciding code is `auto_send_reminders_enabled` in `backend/app/feature_flags.py`... the `is None` check at **line 11** is true, and **line 12 returns `True`**.

report_2.evidence:
Reminder messages **will be sent automatically** for it. The setting defaults to ON when unset.

The deciding code is `auto_send_reminders_enabled(tenant)` in `backend/app/feature_flags.py` (lines 10–13)... the line‑11 condition is true, and **line 12 returns `True`**.

report_1.evidence: This flag governs **outbound patient messaging** (SMS + email), which is the category where null/missing must default to **off**... I would change it: `backend/app/feature_flags.py:12`, `return True` → `return False`.

report_2.evidence: Defaulting to OFF (opt‑in) is the correct behavior for this feature... Where I'd change it: `backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12` — make the unset case return `False`

report_1.evidence: `25509a7` (SLOT-190, Jun 10) created `feature_flags.py` with `compact_calendar_view_enabled`, using `is None → return True`. That grandfather-on default is reasonable there — it's a low-risk UI display preference... a display-preference flag and an outbound-messaging flag sit on opposite sides of the blast-radius line

report_2.evidence: it was **copied verbatim from the calendar-view preference**... introduced `compact_calendar_view_enabled`... The default‑ON was reasonable for `compact_calendar_view` — a cosmetic in-app UI preference where "on when unset" harms nobody. Cloning the identical helper silently carried that "default ON" decision into a feature whose consequence is real outbound patient messaging

report_1.evidence: **Add an env-level master switch and AND it with the per-tenant value.** There is currently **no** master/kill gate — `git grep` and `infra/main.tf` show the only gate is the per-tenant setting. A master switch gives a staged rollout

report_2.evidence: `reminders.py` has only the per-tenant flag: no global kill switch, no environment gating (the sole infra config, `infra/main.tf`, defines an unused `environment` var)... Add operational guardrails to the batch job before wiring a real sender.
